message:
test(qa): validate SLI coverage across scenario matrix and perf contract

Add six new QA policy validation tests that enforce structural integrity
and cross-referencing between the e2e scenario matrix, the perf SLI matrix,
and the benchmark protocol contract.

Why: The new SLI framework (perf_sli_matrix.json) introduces a second
source of truth alongside e2e_scenario_matrix.json. Without automated
cross-validation, it would be easy for these artifacts to drift out of
sync — e.g., a new workflow could be added to the scenario matrix without
SLI coverage, or an SLI could be referenced that doesn't exist in the
catalog. These tests catch that drift at compile time.

New tests:
- scenario_matrix_rows_define_non_empty_sli_ids: Every workflow row must
  include at least one non-empty SLI identifier.
- scenario_matrix_sli_ids_exist_in_perf_sli_catalog: Every SLI referenced
  by a workflow must exist in perf_sli_matrix.json's sli_catalog.
- perf_sli_catalog_entries_have_thresholds_and_user_guidance: Every SLI
  catalog entry must define numeric target/warning/fail thresholds and
  non-empty user_interpretation strings for each level.
- perf_sli_workflow_mapping_covers_scenario_matrix_workflows: Every
  workflow in e2e_scenario_matrix.json must appear in perf_sli_matrix's
  workflow_sli_mapping.
- perf_sli_phase_validation_consumers_include_dependent_beads: The
  phase_validation_consumers array must reference all four required
  downstream bead IDs (bd-3ar8v.1.5, .2.11, .3.11, .6.7).
- perf_sli_matrix_schema_is_versioned: The perf SLI matrix must declare
  a schema field starting with "pi.perf.sli_ux_matrix".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
